body:
ANSWERS TO YOUR THREE QUESTIONS — proceed after reading all of this.

=== 1. THE VALIDATED GATE ===
The gate at line 962 is cleared. Proceed with roadmap items.

=== 2. WHAT 9.38 AND 9.39 CHANGE — the actual spec ===
There is no external spec file. The spec IS the process itself.

The work is this: take every piece of logic in 9.37, one subsystem at a time, and ask — is this the most strategically sound, mathematically coherent, and practically executable version of this logic, given full knowledge of what TUNGSTEN is trying to accomplish as a whole? If the answer is no, make it that. If the answer is yes, leave it and move on.

9.38 = first pass through the full system with that lens. Every weak assumption tightened. Every signal path that could produce a confident output from ambiguous input gated. Every regime transition that was assumed rather than validated, validated. Every function that existed but had never been stress-tested against edge inputs, stress-tested and hardened.

9.39 = second pass. Anything 9.38 couldn't fully resolve — because fixing it properly required seeing the full picture first — gets resolved here. Reinforcement of what 9.38 established. The version where everything that was made better in 9.38 is now made coherent as a whole.

Both versions get the reinforcement and hardening checklist from the prior addendum applied on top of this.

=== 3. THE GROWTH RATE CONSTANTS ===
MED_DAILY_GROWTH_RATE and MED_MEDIAN_RETURN are not capped targets and not cosmetic placeholders. They should be mathematically derived from TUNGSTEN's own competence — meaning: what sustained win rate, at what average RR, with what drawdown envelope, produces a compounding growth rate that is both real and defensible? That number is the correct value. It should not be aspirational. It should not be conservative for conservatism's sake. It should be the honest mathematical output of what a well-calibrated version of this system can actually sustain.

Derive it. If the current 0.42 is defensible given the system's actual logic and realistic win/RR assumptions, keep it. If it's not, replace it with what is. Show your working in the relay message so we can see what you derived and why.

=== PROCEED ===
You have everything you need. Work systematically through the full 9.37 codebase, subsystem by subsystem. 9.38 first, compile clean, then 9.39, compile clean. Apply reinforcement and hardening throughout. Derive the growth rate constants mathematically. Deliver 9.39 compiled clean as a file here with a full description of what changed and why.
